order by o.order_date desc limit 50;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1736821743706,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1738463343706,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"pickup_orders_missing_delivery_addr/02-with_address_join","description":"Pickup orders should have no delivery address — are there any that don't follow this?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Products that have never appeared on an order_items row (LEFT JOIN ... IS NULL pattern)
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, p.product_status, p.date_added
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.products p
LEFT   JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi USING (product_id)
WHERE  oi.product_id IS NULL
ORDER  BY p.date_added DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1724157711091,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1725108111091,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"products_never_ordered/01-canonical_left_join_null","description":"Which products in our catalogue have never been ordered?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Monthly promotion attachment rate (% of orders using a promo)
-- Author: BID

SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date::TIMESTAMP)::DATE AS order_month,
       COUNT(*) AS orders,
       COUNT(promotion_id) AS promoted_orders,
       COUNT(promotion_id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS attachment_rate_pct
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY 1;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1733697311573,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1733697311573,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"name":"promo_attachment_rate_monthly/01-canonical","description":"What is the promotion attachment rate by month?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Attachment rate w/ YoY
WITH monthly AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date::TIMESTAMP)::DATE AS order_month,
           COUNT(promotion_id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS attachment_rate
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT cur.order_month, cur.attachment_rate AS current_rate, prv.attachment_rate AS prior_year_rate,
       cur.attachment_rate - prv.attachment_rate AS yoy_pp_delta
FROM   monthly cur LEFT JOIN monthly prv ON prv.order_month = DATEADD('year', -1, cur.order_month)
ORDER  BY cur.order_month;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1718995169250,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1721500769250,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"promo_attachment_rate_monthly/03-with_yoy_compare","description":"What is the promotion attachment rate by month?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Promotion budget vs realised discount value
-- Author: Sarah Chen — per Promo Attribution runbook, budget != actual discount

WITH actual AS (
    SELECT o.promotion_id,
           SUM((p.list_price - oi.unit_price) * oi.quantity) AS actual_discount
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p  USING (product_id)
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o  USING (order_id)
    WHERE  o.promotion_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT pr.promotion_id, pr.promotion_name, pr.promotion_cost AS allocated_budget,
       a.actual_discount
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions pr
LEFT   JOIN actual a USING (promotion_id)
ORDER  BY pr.promotion_cost DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1728878192248,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1731128192248,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"promo_budget_vs_actual_discount/01-canonical","description":"How does each promotion's allocated budget compare to the actual discount given?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Budget vs actual with variance pct (over- vs under-budget)
WITH actual AS (
    SELECT o.promotion_id, SUM((p.list_price - oi.unit_price) * oi.quantity) AS actual_discount
    FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.products p USING (product_id)
    JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o USING (order_id)
    WHERE o.promotion_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT pr.promotion_name, pr.promotion_cost AS budget, COALESCE(a.actual_discount, 0) AS actual,
       COALESCE(a.actual_discount, 0) - pr.promotion_cost AS variance,
       (COALESCE(a.actual_discount, 0) - pr.promotion_cost) * 100.0 / NULLIF(pr.promotion_cost, 0) AS variance_pct,
       CASE WHEN a.actual_discount > pr.promotion_cost * 1.1 THEN 'over_budget'
            WHEN a.actual_discount < pr.promotion_cost * 0.5 THEN 'under_used'
            ELSE 'on_budget' END AS status
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions pr
LEFT JOIN actual a USING (promotion_id)
ORDER BY ABS(COALESCE(a.actual_discount, 0) - pr.promotion_cost) DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1741646679263,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1742251479263,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"promo_budget_vs_actual_discount/02-with_variance_pct","description":"How does each promotion's allocated budget compare to the actual discount given?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Promotional uplift: revenue during window vs equivalent prior window
-- Author: David Kim
-- Promotion windows that have NULL end_date treated as "still running" — skipping those for the comparison.

WITH active_promos AS (
    SELECT promotion_id, promotion_name,
           promotion_start_date::TIMESTAMP AS promo_start,
           promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP   AS promo_end,
           DATEDIFF('day', promotion_start_date::TIMESTAMP, promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP) AS promo_len_days
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions
    WHERE  promotion_end_date IS NOT NULL
),
during AS (
    SELECT p.promotion_id, SUM(o.order_total) AS revenue_during
    FROM   active_promos p
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o ON o.order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN p.promo_start AND p.promo_end
    GROUP  BY 1
),
before AS (
    SELECT p.promotion_id, SUM(o.order_total) AS revenue_before
    FROM   active_promos p
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o ON o.order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN
               DATEADD('day', -p.promo_len_days, p.promo_start) AND p.promo_start
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT p.promotion_id, p.promotion_name,
       b.revenue_before, d.revenue_during,
       d.revenue_during - b.revenue_before                 AS uplift,
       (d.revenue_during - b.revenue_before) * 100.0
           / NULLIF(b.revenue_before, 0)                   AS uplift_pct
FROM   active_promos p
LEFT   JOIN during d USING (promotion_id)
LEFT   JOIN before b USING (promotion_id)
ORDER  BY uplift DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1720101376550,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1722520576550,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"promo_uplift_baseline_compare/01-canonical_self_join","description":"How much did revenue lift during each promotion vs the same length window before?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Same uplift but only the % column for the slide
-- Author: David Kim

with promos as (
    select promotion_id, promotion_name,
           promotion_start_date::timestamp as ps, promotion_end_date::timestamp as pe,
           datediff('day', promotion_start_date::timestamp, promotion_end_date::timestamp) as length_days
    from order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions
    where promotion_end_date is not null
)
select p.promotion_name,
       (
           select sum(order_total) from order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
           where order_date::timestamp between p.ps and p.pe
       )
       / nullif((
           select sum(order_total) from order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
           where order_date::timestamp between dateadd('day', -p.length_days, p.ps) and p.ps
       ), 0) - 1 as uplift_multiplier
from   promos p
order by uplift_multiplier desc nulls last;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1734761940098,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1734934740098,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"promo_uplift_baseline_compare/02-with_pct_lift","description":"How much did revenue lift during each promotion vs the same length window before?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Uplift only for orders attributed to that promo (more conservative)
-- Author: Sarah Chen — strictly attributed revenue, not all revenue in window

WITH p AS (
    SELECT promotion_id, promotion_name,
           promotion_start_date::TIMESTAMP AS ps, promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP AS pe,
           DATEDIFF('day', promotion_start_date::TIMESTAMP, promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP) AS length_days
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions
    WHERE  promotion_end_date IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT p.promotion_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN o.promotion_id = p.promotion_id
                  AND o.order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN p.ps AND p.pe
                THEN o.order_total ELSE 0 END) AS attributed_revenue_during,
       SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN DATEADD('day', -p.length_days, p.ps) AND p.ps
                THEN o.order_total ELSE 0 END) AS baseline_revenue_before
FROM   p
CROSS  JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o
GROUP  BY p.promotion_name
ORDER  BY attributed_revenue_during DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1720899152600,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1722195152600,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"promo_uplift_baseline_compare/03-by_promo","description":"How much did revenue lift during each promotion vs the same length window before?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Reorder candidates (per (product, warehouse))
-- Author: Ben Porter — feeds the purchase order draft job

SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.restock_level,
       i.restock_level - i.quantity_on_hand AS shortfall
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  i.quantity_on_hand < i.restock_level
ORDER  BY shortfall DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1741912768228,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1743986368228,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"name":"reorder_candidates/01-canonical","description":"Show me products that need reordering (qty below restock level)","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Reorder candidates with suggested reorder quantity
-- Author: Ben Porter — for the planner

SELECT p.product_name,
       w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.restock_level,
       i.reorder_quantity,
       i.max_stock_level,
       LEAST(i.reorder_quantity, i.max_stock_level - i.quantity_on_hand) AS suggested_po_qty
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  i.quantity_on_hand < i.restock_level
ORDER  BY suggested_po_qty DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1733764995744,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1734110595744,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"name":"reorder_candidates/02-with_reorder_qty","description":"Show me products that need reordering (qty below restock level)","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Reorder candidates, excluding suspected CDC anomalies (qty < 0)
-- Author: Andrea Garcia
-- Per the Inventory Staleness runbook, negative on_hand is a known CDC race condition.
-- Excluding from reorder triggers so we don't double-order.

SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       w.warehouse_name,
       i.quantity_on_hand,
       i.restock_level,
       i.restock_level - i.quantity_on_hand AS shortfall
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  i.quantity_on_hand >= 0
  AND  i.quantity_on_hand < i.restock_level
ORDER  BY shortfall DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1722128776503,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1723424776503,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"name":"reorder_candidates/03-exclude_negatives","description":"Show me products that need reordering (qty below restock level)","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Reorder candidates prioritised by shortfall ratio
-- Author: Sarah Chen — ranking which to do first by % below restock

select   p.product_name,
         w.warehouse_name,
         i.quantity_on_hand,
         i.restock_level,
         i.restock_level - i.quantity_on_hand                 as units_short,
         (i.restock_level - i.quantity_on_hand) * 1.0
             / nullif(i.restock_level, 0) * 100               as pct_below_restock,
         i.reorder_quantity
from     order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
join     order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p using (product_id)
join     order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w using (warehouse_id)
where    i.quantity_on_hand < i.restock_level
  and    i.quantity_on_hand >= 0
order by pct_below_restock desc
limit    50
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1745676772733,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1746108772733,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"reorder_candidates/04-prioritised_by_gap","description":"Show me products that need reordering (qty below restock level)","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Repeat-order rate — share of customers with > 1 order
-- Author: PM / Karen

SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_ct > 1 THEN customer_id END) * 100.0
        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id), 0) AS repeat_order_rate_pct,
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id)                   AS total_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_ct > 1 THEN customer_id END) AS repeat_customers
FROM (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_ct
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
)
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1726060797282,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1728393597282,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"name":"repeat_order_rate/01-canonical_with_having","description":"What share of customers have placed more than one order?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Repeat-order rate using a CTE (easier to extend later)
-- Author: Sarah Chen

WITH customer_orders AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
           COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_ct
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
),
totals AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*)                                                AS total_customers,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN order_ct > 1 THEN 1 END)                AS repeat_customers,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN order_ct >= 3 THEN 1 END)               AS three_plus_customers
    FROM   customer_orders
)
SELECT total_customers,
       repeat_customers,
       three_plus_customers,
       repeat_customers      * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_customers, 0) AS repeat_rate_pct,
       three_plus_customers  * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_customers, 0) AS three_plus_rate_pct
FROM   totals
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1735890834918,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1738482834918,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"repeat_order_rate/02-cte_based","description":"What share of customers have placed more than one order?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Repeat-order rate via window function on per-order data
-- Author: David Kim
--
-- Window-function take. Slightly heavier than the CTE version but easier to
-- repurpose for "Nth order metrics" later.

WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
           order_id,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date::TIMESTAMP) AS n
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
)
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id)                          AS total_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN n >= 2 THEN customer_id END) AS repeat_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN n >= 2 THEN customer_id END) * 100.0
        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id), 0)         AS repeat_rate_pct
FROM   ordered
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1724528488528,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1725997288528,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"repeat_order_rate/03-window_function_version","description":"What share of customers have placed more than one order?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Return rate by product category
-- Author: Sarah Chen
-- Definition per Metrics Reference: returned / dispatched.

SELECT c.category_name,
       COUNT(*)                                              AS items_dispatched,
       COUNT(oi.return_date)                                 AS items_returned,
       COUNT(oi.return_date) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)   AS return_rate_pct
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
WHERE  oi.dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY return_rate_pct DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1740168286025,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1742065486025,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"return_rate_by_category/01-canonical_raw","description":"What is the return rate by product category?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Return rate via mart
select category_name,
       count(*) as items_dispatched,
       count(return_date) as items_returned,

{"statement":{"value":"-- Return rate last 90 days only — actionable window
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT c.category_name,
       COUNT(*)                                                AS items_dispatched,
       COUNT(oi.return_date)                                   AS items_returned,
       COUNT(oi.return_date) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)     AS return_rate_pct
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
WHERE  oi.dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('day', -90,
           (SELECT MAX(dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items))
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY return_rate_pct DESC
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Categories ranked by return rate, plus baseline rate for comparison
-- Author: David Kim

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT c.category_name,
           COUNT(*)                AS items_dispatched,
           COUNT(oi.return_date)   AS items_returned
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
    WHERE  oi.dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
    GROUP  BY 1
),
overall AS (
    SELECT SUM(items_returned) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(items_dispatched), 0) AS baseline_rate
    FROM   stats
)
SELECT s.category_name,
       s.items_dispatched,
       s.items_returned,
       s.items_returned * 100.0 / NULLIF(s.items_dispatched, 0) AS category_rate,
       o.baseline_rate,
       (s.items_returned * 100.0 / NULLIF(s.items_dispatched, 0)) - o.baseline_rate AS rate_vs_baseline,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY s.items_returned * 100.0 / NULLIF(s.items_dispatched, 0) DESC) AS rank_by_rate
FROM   stats s, overall o
ORDER  BY rank_by_rate
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1737136171571,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1738345771571,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"return_rate_by_category/04-ranked_categories","description":"What is the return rate by product category?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Return rate within 30 days of dispatch — strict "fast return" window
-- Author: David Kim
-- "Returns within window" is more actionable than lifetime returns because
-- it filters out long-tail returns that may be unrelated to the warehouse.

SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*) AS items_dispatched,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.return_date IS NOT NULL
                   AND DATEDIFF('day', oi.dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP, oi.return_date::TIMESTAMP) <= 30
                  THEN 1 END) AS fast_returns,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.return_date IS NOT NULL
                   AND DATEDIFF('day', oi.dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP, oi.return_date::TIMESTAMP) <= 30
                  THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS fast_return_rate_pct
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o  USING (order_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w  USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  oi.dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY fast_return_rate_pct DESC
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Return rate by channel, with deviation from overall baseline
-- Author: Sarah Chen — for the spotting outliers

with stats as (
    select o.order_mode, count(*) as dispatched, count(oi.return_date) as returned
    from order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    join order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o using (order_id)
    where oi.dispatch_date is not null
    group by 1
)
select order_mode, dispatched, returned,
       returned * 100.0 / nullif(dispatched, 0) as rate_pct,
       (returned * 100.0 / nullif(dispatched, 0))
         - (sum(returned) over () * 100.0 / nullif(sum(dispatched) over (), 0)) as vs_baseline_pp
from stats
order by rate_pct desc;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue by product category — full history
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT c.category_id,
       c.category_name,
       SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS revenue,
       COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id)        AS orders,
       SUM(oi.quantity)                  AS units_sold
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders             o  USING (order_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
WHERE  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY revenue DESC
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue rolled up to top-level category via the parent_category_id tree
-- Author: David Kim — needed this for the category P&L review.
-- product_categories is self-referential. Recursive CTE to find root for each leaf,
-- then sum revenue under each root.

WITH RECURSIVE cat_tree AS (
    SELECT category_id,
           category_name,
           parent_category_id,
           category_id   AS root_id,
           category_name AS root_name,
           1 AS depth
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories
    WHERE  parent_category_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT c.category_id,
           c.category_name,
           c.parent_category_id,
           t.root_id,
           t.root_name,
           t.depth + 1
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c
    JOIN   cat_tree t ON c.parent_category_id = t.category_id
),
product_revenue AS (
    SELECT p.product_id,
           p.category_id,
           SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS rev
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o USING (order_id)
    WHERE  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1, 2
)
SELECT t.root_name           AS top_level_category,
       SUM(pr.rev)            AS revenue,
       COUNT(DISTINCT pr.product_id) AS products,
       MAX(t.depth)           AS deepest_subcategory
FROM   product_revenue pr
JOIN   cat_tree t USING (category_id)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY revenue DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1743822019931,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1745377219931,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"revenue_by_category/04-recursive_category_tree","description":"Break down revenue by product category","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue by customer class — matches the glossary term definition

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue + AOV per class
-- Author: PM / Karen
-- Wanted to know if AOV varies materially by class (it should, intuitively).

select   customer_class,
         count(distinct order_id)                           as orders,
         sum(order_total)                                   as revenue,
         sum(order_total) / count(distinct order_id)        as aov,
         count(distinct customer_id)                        as customers,
         sum(order_total) / count(distinct customer_id)     as avg_revenue_per_customer
from     order_entry_db.analytics.order_details
group by 1
order by aov desc
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue + count + margin contribution by delivery type
-- Author: Sarah Chen
-- Margin contribution = revenue - shipping. Doesn't include COGS — just looks
-- at how much shipping eats into top-line by delivery option.

SELECT delivery_type,
       COUNT(*)                                          AS orders,
       SUM(order_total)                                  AS revenue,
       SUM(cost_of_delivery)                             AS shipping_cost,
       SUM(order_total) - SUM(cost_of_delivery)          AS revenue_after_shipping,
       (SUM(order_total) - SUM(cost_of_delivery))
           / NULLIF(SUM(order_total), 0) * 100            AS pct_kept_after_shipping
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY revenue DESC
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Revenue by payment method

ORDER  BY revenue DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1731345550107,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1732468750107,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"name":"revenue_by_payment_method/01-canonical","description":"What revenue comes from each payment method?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Payment method + AOV
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT payment_method_code,
       COUNT(*)         AS orders,
       SUM(order_total) AS revenue,
       AVG(order_total) AS aov
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY aov DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1725622031273,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1728127631273,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"revenue_by_payment_method/03-with_aov_per_method","description":"What revenue comes from each payment method?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Daily counts last 7 days — spot a sudden drop or gap
SELECT TO_DATE(order_date::TIMESTAMP) AS day, COUNT(*) AS orders
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
WHERE  order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('day', -7,
           (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY 1 DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1721251751600,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1722979751600,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"snowflake_freshness_check/03-with_count_per_day","description":"How fresh is the orders data on Snowflake?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Total stock value by warehouse — using list_price
-- Author: Ben Porter — for the quarterly inventory carry review

SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.product_id)                  AS sku_count,
       SUM(GREATEST(i.quantity_on_hand, 0))          AS total_units,
       SUM(GREATEST(i.quantity_on_hand, 0) * p.list_price) AS stock_value_list
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY stock_value_list DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1747185355755,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1747206000000,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"name":"stock_value_by_warehouse/01-canonical_list_price","description":"What is the total stock value held at each warehouse?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Stock value using min_price (conservative floor)
-- Author: Andrea Garcia — finance prefers a conservative floor over list price.

select w.warehouse_name,
       count(distinct i.product_id)                                 as sku_count,
       sum(greatest(i.quantity_on_hand, 0))                         as total_units,
       sum(greatest(i.quantity_on_hand, 0) * p.min_price)           as conservative_value,
       sum(greatest(i.quantity_on_hand, 0) * p.list_price)          as list_value
from   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
join   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p using (product_id)
join   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w using (warehouse_id)
group by 1
order by conservative_value desc
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1718859401240,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1719032201240,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"name":"stock_value_by_warehouse/02-with_min_price_floor","description":"What is the total stock value held at each warehouse?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Stock value ranking
-- Author: Sarah Chen

WITH wh_value AS (
    SELECT w.warehouse_id,
           w.warehouse_name,
           SUM(GREATEST(i.quantity_on_hand, 0) * p.list_price) AS stock_value
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
    GROUP  BY 1, 2
)
SELECT warehouse_id,
       warehouse_name,
       stock_value,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY stock_value DESC)         AS rank_by_value,
       stock_value * 100.0 / SUM(stock_value) OVER ()  AS pct_of_network
FROM   wh_value
ORDER  BY rank_by_value
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1745087489741,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1747206000000,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"stock_value_by_warehouse/03-ranked_warehouses","description":"What is the total stock value held at each warehouse?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Time-to-return mean
-- Author: David Kim

SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF('day', dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP, return_date::TIMESTAMP)) AS avg_days_to_return,
       COUNT(*) AS returned_items
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items
WHERE  return_date IS NOT NULL AND dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1725235721598,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1726358921598,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"time_to_return_distribution/01-canonical_avg","description":"How long after dispatch do customers typically return items?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Time-to-return distribution
-- Author: David Kim — looking for the "fast return" cliff

with t as (
    select datediff('day', dispatch_date::timestamp, return_date::timestamp) as days_to_return
    from order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items
    where return_date is not null and dispatch_date is not null
)
select count(*) as returns,
       avg(days_to_return)                                       as mean_days,
       median(days_to_return)                                    as p50,
       percentile_cont(0.25) within group (order by days_to_return) as p25,
       percentile_cont(0.75) within group (order by days_to_return) as p75,
       percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by days_to_return) as p95,
       max(days_to_return)                                       as max_days,
       count(case when days_to_return <= 7 then 1 end)           as returns_within_7d,
       count(case when days_to_return <= 30 then 1 end)          as returns_within_30d
from t;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1732410877948,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1732929277948,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"time_to_return_distribution/02-with_percentiles","description":"How long after dispatch do customers typically return items?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Avg days-to-return by category
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT c.category_name,
       COUNT(*) AS returns,
       AVG(DATEDIFF('day', oi.dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP, oi.return_date::TIMESTAMP)) AS avg_days_to_return,
       MEDIAN(DATEDIFF('day', oi.dispatch_date::TIMESTAMP, oi.return_date::TIMESTAMP)) AS median_days
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
WHERE  oi.return_date IS NOT NULL AND oi.dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY avg_days_to_return DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1743311829416,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1745731029416,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"time_to_return_distribution/03-by_category","description":"How long after dispatch do customers typically return items?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top 10 customers by lifetime value (CLV)
-- Author: David Kim — feeds the high-value cohort export for marketing.

SELECT c.customer_id,
       c.cust_first_name || ' ' || c.cust_last_name AS customer_name,
       c.customer_class,
       SUM(o.order_total)         AS lifetime_revenue,
       COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS lifetime_orders
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders    o
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.customers c USING (customer_id)
WHERE  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER  BY lifetime_revenue DESC
LIMIT  10
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1720121810819,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1721158610819,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"top_customers_by_clv/01-canonical","description":"Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime value?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top CLV customers, with tenure
-- Author: David Kim — adds first_order_date to compute "revenue per year on platform"

WITH cust AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
           SUM(order_total)              AS lifetime_revenue,
           COUNT(DISTINCT order_id)      AS lifetime_orders,
           MIN(order_date::TIMESTAMP)    AS first_order_at,
           MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP)    AS most_recent_order
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT c.customer_id,
       cu.cust_first_name || ' ' || cu.cust_last_name AS customer_name,
       cu.customer_class,
       c.lifetime_revenue,
       c.lifetime_orders,
       c.first_order_at,
       c.most_recent_order,
       DATEDIFF('day', c.first_order_at, c.most_recent_order) AS days_on_platform,
       c.lifetime_revenue
           / NULLIF(DATEDIFF('day', c.first_order_at, c.most_recent_order), 0) * 365
                                                       AS annualised_revenue
FROM   cust c
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.customers cu USING (customer_id)
ORDER  BY c.lifetime_revenue DESC
LIMIT  10
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1718496812821,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1719533612821,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"top_customers_by_clv/02-with_first_order_date","description":"Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime value?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- CLV leaderboard + per-customer AOV
-- Author: Sarah Chen — Karen asked whether the top CLV are also high-AOV

select   c.customer_id,
         c.cust_first_name,
         c.cust_last_name,
         c.customer_class,
         sum(o.order_total)                   as clv,
         count(distinct o.order_id)           as orders,
         sum(o.order_total)
           / count(distinct o.order_id)       as aov_per_customer
from     order_entry_db.order_entry.orders    o
join     order_entry_db.order_entry.customers c using (customer_id)
where    o.order_status not in (3, 4)
group by 1, 2, 3, 4
order by clv desc
limit    10
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1726065010227,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1728225010227,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_customers_by_clv/03-with_avg_order_value","description":"Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime value?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top B2C customers by CLV (excludes accounts assigned to a sales rep)
-- Author: Karen Okonkwo — for the consumer marketing campaign list

SELECT c.customer_id,
       c.cust_first_name || ' ' || c.cust_last_name AS customer_name,
       c.customer_class,
       SUM(o.order_total)         AS lifetime_revenue,
       COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS lifetime_orders
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders    o
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.customers c USING (customer_id)
WHERE  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
  AND  c.account_mgr_id IS NULL     -- B2C only — no assigned sales rep
GROUP  BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER  BY lifetime_revenue DESC
LIMIT  10
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1741027513750,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1741801513750,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"name":"top_customers_by_clv/04-exclude_b2b_accounts","description":"Who are our top 10 customers by lifetime value?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products by absolute return count
SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS returns
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
WHERE  oi.return_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY returns DESC
LIMIT  25;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1730605232980,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1733200832980,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_returns/01-canonical_count","description":"Which products are returned most often?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products by return rate (not raw count)
-- Author: Sarah Chen — raw count favours high-volume SKUs; this is normalised

WITH stats AS (
    SELECT p.product_id,
           p.product_name,
           COUNT(*)              AS dispatched,
           COUNT(oi.return_date) AS returned
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
    WHERE  oi.dispatch_date IS NOT NULL
    GROUP  BY 1, 2
)
SELECT product_id, product_name, dispatched, returned,
       returned * 100.0 / NULLIF(dispatched, 0) AS return_rate_pct
FROM   stats
WHERE  dispatched >= 10              -- need volume floor for a meaningful rate
ORDER  BY return_rate_pct DESC
LIMIT  25
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1739455914588,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1741612314588,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_returns/02-with_return_rate","description":"Which products are returned most often?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products by returns, with category context
-- Author: Sarah Chen

SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, c.category_name,
       COUNT(*)                                AS returns,
       SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity)        AS returned_revenue
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items        oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products           p  USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c  USING (category_id)
WHERE  oi.return_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP  BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER  BY returns DESC
LIMIT  25
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1732440520518,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1732958920518,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_returns/04-by_category","description":"Which products are returned most often?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top 20 products by revenue — last full quarter
-- Author: Sarah Chen
-- Revenue uses order_items.unit_price (post-discount), NOT products.list_price.

WITH q_bounds AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP))                   AS this_q_start,
           DATEADD('quarter', -1, DATE_TRUNC('quarter', MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP))) AS last_q_start
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
)
SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS revenue,
       COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id)      AS orders,
       SUM(oi.quantity)                 AS units_sold
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o  USING (order_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p  USING (product_id)
CROSS  JOIN q_bounds q
WHERE  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= q.last_q_start
  AND  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP <  q.this_q_start
  AND  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY revenue DESC
LIMIT  20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1726971440188,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1729563440188,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_revenue/01-canonical_join_chain","description":"Show me the top 20 products by revenue last quarter","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top 20 products by revenue, last quarter — via the mart
-- order_details has line_total pre-computed.

with q as (
    select date_trunc('quarter', max(order_date::timestamp))                          as this_q_start,
           dateadd('quarter', -1, date_trunc('quarter', max(order_date::timestamp)))  as last_q_start
    from   order_entry_db.analytics.order_details
)
select   product_id,
         product_name,
         sum(line_total)             as revenue,
         count(distinct order_id)    as orders,
         sum(quantity)               as units_sold
from     order_entry_db.analytics.order_details od, q
where    order_date::timestamp >= q.last_q_start
  and    order_date::timestamp <  q.this_q_start
  and    order_status not in (3, 4)
group by 1, 2
order by revenue desc
limit    20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1720117434491,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1722104634491,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_revenue/02-mart_version","description":"Show me the top 20 products by revenue last quarter","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products by revenue — quick version
-- Author: Karen Okonkwo
-- !! finance flagged this one: list_price ignores discounts so this OVERSTATES
-- revenue on promoted lines. Should be order_items.unit_price. Leaving on the
-- wiki as "do not use" example for now.

SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       SUM(p.list_price * oi.quantity) AS revenue_overstated
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o USING (order_id)
WHERE  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('quarter', -1,
             (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY revenue_overstated DESC
LIMIT  20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1734038006577,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1736025206577,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_revenue/03-list_price_anti","description":"Show me the top 20 products by revenue last quarter","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.kirk@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top revenue products grouped by category for the merch review
-- Author: Sarah Chen — rolls up by category, ranks within

WITH revenue AS (
    SELECT p.product_id,
           p.product_name,
           p.category_id,
           SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS rev
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
    JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders      o USING (order_id)
    WHERE  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('quarter', -1,
               (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
      AND  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1, 2, 3
)
SELECT c.category_name,
       r.product_id,
       r.product_name,
       r.rev,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_name ORDER BY r.rev DESC) AS rank_in_cat
FROM   revenue r
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.product_categories c USING (category_id)
QUALIFY rank_in_cat <= 3            -- top 3 per category
ORDER  BY c.category_name, rank_in_cat
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1727457586702,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1727543986702,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_products_by_revenue/04-with_category_grouping","description":"Show me the top 20 products by revenue last quarter","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top promotions by attributed revenue
-- Author: Sarah Chen (Senior Data Analyst)
--
-- Reference shape from the Promotion Attribution runbook. LEFT JOIN from orders
-- so the same scaffold can be reused for a baseline ("how much revenue is
-- *not* attributed to a promo") without rewriting the join. For this specific
-- question we just filter to promoted rows in the final WHERE.

SELECT p.promotion_id,
       p.promotion_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id)              AS orders_attributed,
       SUM(o.order_total)                      AS attributed_revenue,
       AVG(o.order_total)                      AS aov_on_promo,
       SUM(o.order_total) * 100.0
           / SUM(SUM(o.order_total)) OVER ()   AS pct_of_promoted_revenue
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders       o
LEFT   JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions p
       ON   o.promotion_id = p.promotion_id
WHERE  o.promotion_id IS NOT NULL                -- focus on promoted orders
  AND  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)              -- exclude held / cancelled
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY attributed_revenue DESC
LIMIT  20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1735245890405,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1736628290405,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"top_promos_by_revenue/01-canonical_left_join","description":"Which promotions generated the most revenue?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top promos by revenue (promotion-driven join)
-- Author: James Wilson (BI Developer)
--
-- Drives the join from the promotions table, INNER joining orders.
-- Works for this question because we *want* only promoted orders, but the
-- runbook flags this shape as a footgun: if someone reuses it for a baseline
-- ("revenue with vs without promotion") the null-promo orders get dropped.

SELECT   p.promotion_id,
         p.promotion_name,
         COUNT(o.order_id)        AS orders_attributed,
         SUM(o.order_total)       AS attributed_revenue
FROM     order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions p
INNER JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders   o
        ON p.promotion_id = o.promotion_id
WHERE    o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY attributed_revenue DESC
LIMIT    20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1744886671018,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1746269071018,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"name":"top_promos_by_revenue/02-inner_join_anti","description":"Which promotions generated the most revenue?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top promos by revenue, attributed only within active promo window
-- Author: David Kim (Data Scientist)
--
-- Per the Promo Attribution runbook step 2: enforce that the order_date falls
-- inside [promotion_start_date, promotion_end_date]. Catches data quality
-- issues where promotion_id is set on orders that pre-date or post-date the
-- campaign. Treats NULL end_date as "still active" so we don't drop ongoing
-- campaign attributions.

WITH promo_attribution AS (
    SELECT  o.order_id,
            o.order_total,
            o.order_date::TIMESTAMP                     AS order_ts,
            p.promotion_id,
            p.promotion_name,
            p.promotion_start_date::TIMESTAMP           AS promo_start,
            COALESCE(p.promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP,
                     '9999-12-31'::TIMESTAMP)           AS promo_end
    FROM    order_entry_db.order_entry.orders     o
    JOIN    order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions p ON o.promotion_id = p.promotion_id
    WHERE   o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
)
SELECT promotion_id,
       promotion_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT order_id)  AS orders_in_window,
       SUM(order_total)          AS revenue_in_window,
       SUM(CASE WHEN order_ts NOT BETWEEN promo_start AND promo_end
                THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS orders_outside_window      -- data-quality canary
FROM   promo_attribution
WHERE  order_ts BETWEEN promo_start AND promo_end
GROUP  BY 1, 2
ORDER  BY revenue_in_window DESC
LIMIT  20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1722964328502,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1725556328502,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"},"name":"top_promos_by_revenue/03-with_attribution_window","description":"Which promotions generated the most revenue?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.brock1@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top promos by revenue — mart shortcut
-- Author: James Wilson (BI Developer)
--
-- analytics.order_details has promotion_name pre-resolved + the discount columns.
-- Grain is line-item; dedupe via the order header before summing.

with order_header as (
    select   order_id,
             any_value(promotion_id)    as promotion_id,
             any_value(promotion_name)  as promotion_name,
             any_value(order_total)     as order_total,
             any_value(order_status)    as order_status
    from     order_entry_db.analytics.order_details
    group by order_id
)
select   promotion_id,
         promotion_name,
         count(*)                       as orders_attributed,
         sum(order_total)               as attributed_revenue,
         avg(order_total)               as aov_on_promo
from     order_header
where    promotion_id is not null
  and    order_status not in (3, 4)
group by 1, 2
order by attributed_revenue desc
limit    20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1733049235252,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1735382035252,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"},"name":"top_promos_by_revenue/04-mart_version","description":"Which promotions generated the most revenue?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.jonny2@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Warehouse throughput, last 90 days
-- Author: Ben Porter

SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*)         AS orders,
       SUM(o.order_total) AS revenue
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders     o
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('day', -90,
           (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY orders DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1723781207117,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1725422807117,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"},"name":"warehouse_throughput/01-canonical","description":"Which warehouses fulfilled the most orders in the last 90 days?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.bryan@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Warehouse throughput via mart
-- Author: James Wilson

with hdr as (
    select order_id, any_value(warehouse_name) as wh, any_value(order_total) as total, any_value(order_date) as dt
    from order_entry_db.analytics.order_details group by order_id
)
select wh as warehouse_name, count(*) as orders, sum(total) as revenue
from hdr
where dt::timestamp >= dateadd('day', -90, (select max(dt::timestamp) from hdr))
group by 1 order by orders desc;

{"statement":{"value":"-- Warehouse throughput + revenue/order
-- Author: Sarah Chen — comparing the fulfillment mix

SELECT w.warehouse_name,
       COUNT(*)                          AS orders,
       SUM(o.order_total)                AS revenue,
       AVG(o.order_total)                AS aov,
       SUM(o.cost_of_delivery)           AS shipping_costs,
       SUM(o.cost_of_delivery) / COUNT(*) AS avg_shipping_per_order
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders     o
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE  o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('day', -90,
           (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
  AND  o.order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
GROUP  BY 1
ORDER  BY revenue DESC
;

{"statement":{"value":"-- WoW revenue change using LAG
-- Author: Sarah Chen — weekly trend tile

WITH weekly AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', order_date::TIMESTAMP)::DATE AS week_start,
           SUM(order_total)                                AS revenue,
           COUNT(*)                                        AS orders
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT week_start,
       revenue,
       orders,
       LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY week_start)               AS prior_week_revenue,
       revenue - LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY week_start)     AS wow_delta,
       (revenue - LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY week_start))
           / NULLIF(LAG(revenue, 1) OVER (ORDER BY week_start), 0) * 100 AS wow_pct
FROM   weekly
ORDER  BY week_start
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1725518711014,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1727160311014,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"wow_revenue_change/01-canonical_lag","description":"How did revenue change week-over-week?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- WoW via self-join — older style but easier to read for some folks
-- Author: Andrea Garcia

WITH weekly AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', order_date::TIMESTAMP)::DATE AS week_start,
           SUM(order_total)                                AS revenue
    FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
    WHERE  order_status NOT IN (3, 4)
    GROUP  BY 1
)
SELECT cur.week_start,
       cur.revenue                            AS current_revenue,
       prv.revenue                            AS prior_week_revenue,
       cur.revenue - prv.revenue              AS wow_delta,
       (cur.revenue - prv.revenue) / NULLIF(prv.revenue, 0) * 100 AS wow_pct
FROM   weekly cur
LEFT   JOIN weekly prv
       ON   prv.week_start = DATEADD('week', -1, cur.week_start)
ORDER  BY cur.week_start
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1719578215314,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1719751015314,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"name":"wow_revenue_change/02-self_join_version","description":"How did revenue change week-over-week?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Dormant customers (no orders >180 days)
SELECT c.customer_id, c.cust_first_name, c.cust_last_name,
       MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order,
       DATEDIFF('day', MAX(o.order_date::TIMESTAMP),
                (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders)) AS days_since_last
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.customers c
LEFT JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o USING (customer_id)
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
HAVING DATEDIFF('day', MAX(o.order_date::TIMESTAMP),
                (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders)) > 180
ORDER BY days_since_last DESC LIMIT 30;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1743732460000,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1744682860000,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/cohort_dormant_customers","description":"Dormant customers (no orders >180 days)","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Pulling the full order history for customer 963 (Laure Schruurs).
-- CX got a complaint about a missed delivery — need to confirm what she's
-- ordered recently and whether any are still open.
-- Author: Sarah Chen — won't need this query again after this ticket.

SELECT o.order_id,
       o.order_date,
       o.order_status,
       o.order_total,
       o.delivery_type,
       o.cost_of_delivery,
       w.warehouse_name,
       p.promotion_name
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders     o
LEFT   JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses w  USING (warehouse_id)
LEFT   JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions p  USING (promotion_id)
WHERE  o.customer_id = 963
ORDER  BY o.order_date::TIMESTAMP DESC
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1740001770245,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1742503770245,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/customer_lookup_963_complaint","description":"Pulling the full order history for customer 963 (Laure Schruurs).","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Quick check: customer emails are supposed to be unique per the docs.
-- Are they actually? Running before the export to the new CRM.
-- Author: Julia Novak

select   cust_email,
         count(*) as customer_rows,
         min(customer_id) as first_seen_id,
         max(customer_id) as latest_seen_id
from     order_entry_db.order_entry.customers

{"statement":{"value":"-- Feb 2025 came in soft — exec asked why. Pulling daily revenue + order counts
-- for the month to see if it was one bad week or a broader dip.
-- Anchored to Feb only, not parameterising — this is throwaway.

SELECT TO_DATE(order_date::TIMESTAMP) AS order_day,
       COUNT(*)                       AS orders,
       SUM(order_total)               AS revenue,
       AVG(order_total)               AS aov
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.orders

{"statement":{"value":"-- Inventory health: how many (product, wh) pairs in each state?
SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN quantity_on_hand = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS oos_pairs,
    SUM(CASE WHEN quantity_on_hand BETWEEN 1 AND restock_level - 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS low,
    SUM(CASE WHEN quantity_on_hand >= restock_level THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS healthy,
    SUM(CASE WHEN quantity_on_hand > max_stock_level THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS overstocked
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1726450544389,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1726623344389,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/ops_inventory_health_summary","description":"Inventory health: how many (product, wh) pairs in each state?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Promotions ending in the next 30 days
SELECT promotion_name, promotion_end_date,
       DATEDIFF('day', (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders), promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP) AS days_left
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.promotions
WHERE promotion_end_date IS NOT NULL
  AND promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP > (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders)
  AND promotion_end_date::TIMESTAMP < DATEADD('day', 30, (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
ORDER BY days_left;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1746253359790,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1746944559790,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/ops_promos_ending_soon","description":"Promotions ending in the next 30 days","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Dispatches >7 days from order, last 30d

{"statement":{"value":"-- Why is product 842 (Smart Wi-Fi Light Bulbs) showing "Out of Stock" status
-- in the catalogue but inventories still has positive stock? Sales rep flagged
-- it after a customer was told it was unavailable but our system showed 4500+
-- units across warehouses. Want to confirm before pinging merchandising.
-- Author: Andrea Garcia

SELECT
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name,
    p.product_status,
    p.list_price,
    p.date_added,
    i.warehouse_id,
    w.warehouse_name,
    i.quantity_on_hand,
    i.restock_level
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p
JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i USING (product_id)
JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses  w USING (warehouse_id)
WHERE p.product_id = 842
ORDER BY i.quantity_on_hand DESC;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1741275119914,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1743863519914,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"},"name":"noise/product_842_wifi_bulbs_investigation","description":"Why is product 842 (Smart Wi-Fi Light Bulbs) showing "Out of Stock" status","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.alex@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Invariant check: min_price should always be <= list_price.
-- Spotting any inverted entries.
-- Author: Julia (DQE)

SELECT product_id, product_name, list_price, min_price, min_price - list_price AS gap

{"statement":{"value":"-- Which products have only ever sold in non-new condition?
-- Trying to identify the marketplace-only catalogue.

SELECT p.product_id,
       p.product_name,
       COUNT(*) AS line_items,
       LISTAGG(DISTINCT oi.condition, ', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY oi.condition) AS conditions_seen
FROM   order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN   order_entry_db.order_entry.products    p USING (product_id)
GROUP  BY 1, 2
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN oi.condition = 'New' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0
ORDER  BY line_items DESC
LIMIT  20
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1740044549149,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1742114549149,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/refurbished_only_products","description":"Which products have only ever sold in non-new condition?","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Eastside Logistics Center current load + stock
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS orders_30d,
       COUNT(DISTINCT i.product_id) AS distinct_skus,
       SUM(GREATEST(i.quantity_on_hand, 0)) AS total_units_on_hand
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.warehouses w
LEFT JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o ON o.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
  AND o.order_date::TIMESTAMP >= DATEADD('day', -30, (SELECT MAX(order_date::TIMESTAMP) FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders))
LEFT JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.inventories i ON i.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id
WHERE w.warehouse_name = 'Eastside Logistics Center';
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1740167849190,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1741377449190,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/warehouse_eastside_logistics_center_current_load","description":"Eastside Logistics Center current load + stock","origin":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"}

{"statement":{"value":"-- Quick — do weekend orders skew differently in mode mix? (Curious from ops chat)
select   case when dayofweek(order_date::timestamp) in (0, 6) then 'weekend' else 'weekday' end as period,
         order_mode,
         count(*) as orders,
         avg(order_total) as aov
from     order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
group by 1, 2
order by 1, orders desc
;
","language":"SQL"},"source":"MANUAL","created":{"time":1739834259457,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"lastModified":{"time":1741130259457,"actor":"urn:li:corpuser:b2fd91.sam@example.com"},"name":"noise/weekend_orders_pattern","description":"Quick — do weekend orders skew differently in mode mix? {"statement":{"value":"-- Daily detail for window: december_2024
SELECT TO_DATE(order_date::TIMESTAMP) AS day,
       COUNT(*) AS orders, SUM(order_total) AS revenue, AVG(order_total) AS aov
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.orders
WHERE order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN '2024-12-01' AND '2024-12-31 23:59:59'

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products during december_2024

{"statement":{"value":"-- Top products during november_2024
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(*) AS line_items, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS revenue
FROM order_entry_db.order_entry.order_items oi
JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.orders o USING (order_id)
JOIN order_entry_db.order_entry.products p USING (product_id)
WHERE o.order_date::TIMESTAMP BETWEEN '2024-11-01' AND '2024-11-30 23:59:59'
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 15;
